key_insight: FraminghamPACE is a new DNA methylation “speedometer” designed to estimate the Pace of Aging in mixed-age cohorts and better support healthspan research.
clinical_relevance: It may help identify biomarker surrogate endpoints for geroscience trials by predicting healthspan and detecting response to geroprotective interventions.
method: Adapted a Pace of Aging machine-learning framework for cohorts with variable follow-up.
Built the biomarker using Framingham Heart Study Offspring Cohort DNA methylation data.
Evaluated performance across four independent cohorts and one clinical trial.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports validation across four external cohorts plus one clinical trial, supporting both predictive value for healthspan and responsiveness to intervention.
watch_item: As a new biomarker, it still needs careful validation across more diverse populations and trial settings before broad implementation.
paper_title: An epigenetic speedometer to measure Pace of Aging: FraminghamPACE
